reasoning: Sentimental Value has officially been nominated for Best Film Editing at the 98th Academy Awards, along with F1, Marty Supreme, One Battle After Another, and Sinners. Since it was nominated, the market will not immediately resolve NO. However, as an international family drama directed by Joachim Trier, it is highly uncharacteristic of expected Best Film Editing winners. This category is typically dominated by either the Best Picture front-runner (e.g., One Battle After Another or Sinners) or technically complex, fast-paced action/sound-heavy films (e.g., F1). Prediction markets priced it at 0.00 just prior to nominations, reflecting its extreme long-shot status. Considering its stiff competition and historical Oscar trends, its chances of winning are extremely low, firmly placing it at the bottom tier of contenders (roughly a 3-5% chance of an upset).
